risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

listitem.para: a. When multiple symptoms are listed as chief complaints during the ED triage process, the physician needs to designate only 1 sign, symptom, disease, or injury as the chief complaint (principal reason for the visit). This diagnosis is used to help support the urgency of the ED visit under prudent layperson guidelines as well as support resource utilization.

d. Report one of the following codes from category Z04 for an encounter at which neglect, abuse, or exploitation is suspected but ruled out. However, report codes for the problems addressed in lieu of a category Z04 code when maltreatment is ruled outbut problems are addressed.
c. The physician's dated and timed order for observation status is the starting point of the observation service.

d. All of the above. A physician or QHP providing ED care does not report a procedure that is performed by hospital personnel under general supervision or the global or technical component code for services that include use of hospital equipment. However, the professional component, such as interpretation and report of radiographic findings, may be reported by appending modifier 26 [professional component] to the procedure code.

c. CPT instructs that 99292 be reported in conjunction with 99291 when critical care time extends up to 30 minutes beyond the first 74 minutes and for each additional period of up to 30 minutes. Report 1 unit of 99292 for critical care time of 75 to 104 minutes and then an additional unit for each additional period of up to 30 minutes.
